title: Probe direct next-lap PitStop shift
summary: Because the target is PitNextLap and the table includes PitStop for each row, rows may encode the label as the following same-driver same-race PitStop.
rationale: Because the target is PitNextLap and the table includes PitStop for each row, rows may encode the label as the following same-driver same-race PitStop. This is a classic temporal leakage trap, but in Kaggle test tables it can also be exploitable if full future rows are provided.
implementation_hint: Sort train and test separately by Race_Year/Race, Driver, LapNumber, id; create LeadPitStop, NextLapGap, SameDriverRaceNextRow and EndOfSequence flags. Validate the identity on train, then test a submission that blends model probability with LeadPitStop where the next row is unambiguous.
expected_effect: Potentially very large public AUC gain if the table contains complete lap sequences; also clarifies whether current CV is underusing deterministic structure.
risk: May be considered future-information leakage and may fail at race/driver boundaries, missing laps, or if public/private test rows are not complete sequences.
